author: Jordan (Proxy Interface Agent)
description: The Governor is the policy evaluation and approval authority of the RIO system. It receives structured intents from the Observer (Mantis), evaluates them against the active PolicyBundle, computes risk tiers and blast radius, and renders governance decisions (AUTO_APPROVE, AUTO_DENY, REQUIRE_HUMAN). The Governor holds the approval authority but cannot execute any action. It enforces the four core invariants: Agency, Sovereignty, Truth, and Precision.

role_in_three_power_separation.principle: The Governor is the decision authority in the Three-Power Separation architecture. It evaluates policy and renders governance decisions but cannot observe raw signals (that is Mantis) and cannot execute actions (that is the Executor). The Governor holds OAuth tokens and approval signatures but can only authorize — never act.
role_in_three_power_separation.boundary: The Governor operates between the Observer and the Executor. It receives validated intents from Mantis and issues signed approvals to the Executor. It cannot ingest raw signals, cannot execute tools, and cannot write execution receipts to the ledger.

core_invariants.rule: Human remains final owner and initiator. No action without explicit or pre-approved intent.
Owner controls policy file and cryptographic keys. No external override.
Every action produces verifiable cryptographic receipt. Nothing hidden or altered.
Apply exact friction needed (never more, never less) via policy object and informed consent.

capabilities: Evaluate intents against the active PolicyBundle
Compute risk tiers (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL) based on action type and tool policies
Compute blast radius (financial impact, data domains affected, reversibility)
Render governance decisions: AUTO_APPROVE, AUTO_DENY, REQUIRE_HUMAN
Present informed consent screen when REQUIRE_HUMAN or ambiguity_score > 70
Process human approval/denial with Ed25519 signature verification
Manage policy lifecycle (create, update, version) with Admin signature requirement
Enforce role-based access control (Admin, Approver, Auditor, Policy Manager)
Score ambiguity on 0-100 scale to determine governance routing
Feed governance decision patterns to the Learning Loop for policy improvement proposals
prohibitions: Cannot execute any external action or tool
Cannot observe or ingest raw signals (that is the Observer's role)
Cannot write execution receipts to the ledger (that is the Executor's role)
Cannot self-authorize — no agent can declare its own action approved
Cannot bypass the informed consent gate for REQUIRE_HUMAN decisions
Cannot modify invariants (Agency, Sovereignty, Truth, Precision are immutable)
Cannot issue approvals without a valid Ed25519 signature from an authorized signer
Cannot extend approval expiry or increase maxExecutions after issuance

governance_states.description: Action matches a pre-approved policy rule. No human intervention required.
governance_states.constraints: Only applies to actions explicitly listed in the PolicyBundle's AUTO_APPROVE category. Risk tier must be LOW.

governance_states.description: Action requires explicit human approval before execution can proceed.
examples: Any execution with spend or external impact
governance_states.constraints: Triggered when risk tier is MEDIUM or above, or ambiguity_score > 70. Human must sign approval with Ed25519.

governance_states.description: Action violates core invariants or is explicitly prohibited. Rejected immediately.
governance_states.constraints: Cannot be overridden. Denial is logged in the ledger with reason code.

policy_engine.description: The Policy Engine is the Governor's core evaluation mechanism. It loads the active MasterSeed and PolicyBundle, then evaluates each intent against the defined rules.

components.name: Policy Loader
components.function: Load the active MasterSeed and PolicyBundle into memory or fast cache

components.name: Risk Computation (computeRisk)
components.function: Match the requested toolName against ToolPolicy entries. If no explicit policy exists, default to HIGH (fail-closed).

components.name: Blast Radius Computation (computeBlastRadius)
components.function: Analyze toolArgs to estimate financial impact, data domains affected, and reversibility.
outputs: { financialImpact, dataDomainsAffected, reversibility: HIGH | LOW | NONE }

components.name: Ambiguity Scorer
components.function: Score the intent on a 0-100 scale based on match to policy and historical patterns. High ambiguity routes to REQUIRE_HUMAN.

role_based_access_control.description: The Governor enforces RBAC for all governance operations. Each role has specific capabilities and constraints.

enforcement_gates.check: Does the intent match an allowed action category in the active PolicyBundle?
enforcement_gates.fail_mode: CLOSED — if no matching policy, default to REQUIRE_HUMAN (not AUTO_DENY, to preserve Agency)

enforcement_gates.check: What risk tier does this action fall into? Match toolName against ToolPolicy.
enforcement_gates.fail_mode: CLOSED — if no explicit policy exists, default to HIGH risk

enforcement_gates.check: Does this risk tier require human approval? MEDIUM and above require REQUIRE_HUMAN.
enforcement_gates.fail_mode: CLOSED — cannot proceed to execution without governance decision

enforcement_gates.check: Is the approval cryptographically signed by an authorized signer (Ed25519)?
enforcement_gates.fail_mode: CLOSED — unsigned or invalid signatures are rejected

enforcement_gates.check: Is the approval still within its time window (expiresAt)?
enforcement_gates.fail_mode: CLOSED — expired approvals cannot authorize execution

enforcement_gates.check: Is the ambiguity score above the threshold (default 70)?
enforcement_gates.fail_mode: ESCALATE — route to REQUIRE_HUMAN regardless of policy match

api_contracts.description: Receive a structured intent from the Observer. Evaluate against PolicyBundle. Return governance decision.
api_contracts.request_body: intake-schema.json envelope with identity, intent, context, authorization=null
processing: Extract userId and proxyUserId from authenticated session
Persist Intent with status PENDING_APPROVAL or AUTO_APPROVED or DENIED
api_contracts.response: { intent_id, status, riskTier, blastRadius, governance_decision, timestamp }

api_contracts.description: Process human approval or denial for a pending intent. Requires Ed25519 signature.
processing: If deny: persist Approval with status REJECTED, update Intent
If approve: normalize toolArgs, compute toolArgsHash (SHA-256)
Set maxExecutions based on risk tier (e.g., 1 for HIGH)
Set expiresAt based on risk tier timeout
Verify Ed25519 signature against registered SignerId
Persist Approval, update Intent status to APPROVED
api_contracts.response: { approval_id, intent_id, status, toolArgsHash, expiresAt, maxExecutions }

api_contracts.description: Retrieve the current active PolicyBundle with its linked MasterSeed reference.
api_contracts.response: { policyId, seedId, version, riskTiers, toolPolicies, allowedActions, updated_at }

api_contracts.description: Update the active PolicyBundle. Requires Admin role and Ed25519 signature.
api_contracts.constraints: Cannot modify invariants. Cannot reduce approval requirements below REQUIRE_HUMAN for HIGH risk. All changes logged in ledger.

api_contracts.description: List all intents awaiting human approval (REQUIRE_HUMAN decisions).
api_contracts.response: Array of pending intent objects with risk assessment and blast radius

approval_token_spec.description: When the Governor issues an approval, it creates a time-bound, execution-limited token that the Executor must present to proceed.

fields.approval_id: UUID — unique identifier for this approval
fields.intent_id: UUID — the intent this approval authorizes
fields.signer_id: string — the Ed25519 public key fingerprint of the approver
fields.toolName: string — the specific tool authorized for execution
fields.toolArgsHash: SHA-256 hash of the normalized tool arguments
fields.maxExecutions: integer — maximum number of times this approval can be used (typically 1)
fields.executionsUsed: integer — counter incremented by Executor on each use
fields.expiresAt: ISO 8601 timestamp — approval expires after this time
fields.signature: Ed25519 signature over the canonical form of this approval

invariants: Single-use by default for HIGH risk (maxExecutions = 1)
Cannot be extended or renewed — new approval required
Executor must verify signature before accepting
Executor must verify toolArgsHash matches actual execution arguments

informed_consent_screen.description: When the Governor renders a REQUIRE_HUMAN decision, it presents an informed consent screen to the human approver.
displayed_information: Intent summary (what the action will do)
Historical context (similar past actions and their outcomes)
Time constraint (how long the approval will be valid)
trigger_conditions: Action involves external impact (send, spend, delete)
First-time action type with no policy precedent

relationship_to_other_components.observer_mantis: The Governor receives validated intents from Mantis. Mantis translates raw signals into structured envelopes; the Governor evaluates them against policy. The Governor has no influence on how Mantis observes or translates.
relationship_to_other_components.executor: The Governor issues signed approval tokens to the Executor. The Executor must present a valid, unexpired, signature-verified approval before executing any action. The Governor cannot execute actions directly.
relationship_to_other_components.ledger: The Governor writes INTENT_CREATED and APPROVAL_CREATED entries to the ledger. It does not write execution receipts (that is the Executor's responsibility). The Governor reads ledger entries for policy improvement via the Learning Loop.
relationship_to_other_components.learning_loop: The Governor feeds governance decision patterns to the Learning Loop, which proposes policy updates. Proposed updates must go through governance (Admin signature) before activation — the Learning Loop cannot self-modify policy.
relationship_to_other_components.kill_switch: The Governor respects the kill switch state. If the proxy is killed, the Governor rejects all new intents with reason PROXY_KILLED. The kill switch is activated via the Executor's /api/kill endpoint.

invariants: No action proceeds without a governance decision (fail-closed)
No agent can self-authorize — approval requires a different identity than the requester
All governance decisions are logged in the tamper-evident ledger
Policy changes require Admin role and Ed25519 signature
Core invariants (Agency, Sovereignty, Truth, Precision) are immutable
If no explicit policy exists for an action, default to HIGH risk (fail-closed)
The Governor cannot execute any external action
Ambiguity above threshold always routes to REQUIRE_HUMAN
